spec.reasoning: Three safe anchor types are used. (1) Lexical: All column names are renamed — 'unit1'–'unit5' become 'block1'–'block5' and 'residuals' becomes 'remainders'. These are semantically equivalent at the same descriptiveness level (abbreviation+number to abbreviation+number, full word to full word). The word 'unit' does appear in the instruction but only as a reference to the column names ('unit 1 to unit 5', 'unit column'), so renaming them is valid and the instruction references must be updated by the user accordingly. 'residuals' appears in the instruction as a column reference so renaming to 'remainders' is appropriate. (2) Positional title row insert: A title row is inserted at row 1, pushing headers to row 2 and data down by one row. This tests whether the model hardcodes row positions. The task does not delete/insert rows itself — it modifies cell values in place — so this is safe. The answer position shifts from A1:I21 to A1:I22. (3) Distractor column: 'block_total' is added after the last column. It is plausibly related (sounds like a sum of block columns) but is clearly different from any existing column, testing whether the model incorrectly incorporates it.
